**PRODUCT REQUIREMENT DOCUMENT**

**SafeSight AI**

AI-Powered Construction Site Safety Monitoring & Alert System

| **Document Version** | 1.0                       |
| -------------------- | ------------------------- |
| **Status**           | Final Draft               |
| **Date**             | April 2026                |
| **Classification**   | Confidential              |
| **Prepared by**      | SafeSight AI Product Team |

# **1\. Executive Summary**

SafeSight AI is a full-stack, AI-driven construction site safety monitoring and alerting platform. The system leverages state-of-the-art computer vision and deep learning models (YOLOv8/v9, ResNet, EfficientDet) to continuously analyze live video feeds from existing IP cameras installed on construction sites. It automatically detects Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) compliance, identifies hazardous zone violations, recognizes dangerous behaviors, and delivers real-time multi-channel alerts to site supervisors, safety officers, and executive management.

The platform is designed as a complete production-ready product - not a prototype or MVP - covering the full software lifecycle from AI inference engine and edge computing layer to cloud backend, web/mobile management portal, analytics dashboard, ERP/BIM integrations, and regulatory compliance reporting.

| **Key Value Proposition**                                                                                       |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Reduce PPE non-compliance incidents by up to 85% through real-time AI detection and instant alerting            |
| Lower construction site accident rates and associated costs (compensation, project delays, reputational damage) |
| Provide evidence-based safety management with immutable audit trails for regulatory bodies                      |
| Deliver ROI within 6-12 months by reducing insurance premiums, fines, and accident-related costs                |
| Integrate seamlessly with existing camera infrastructure - no new hardware investment required                  |

## **1.1 Problem Statement**

Construction sites remain among the most hazardous work environments globally. The construction sector accounts for approximately 20% of all fatal workplace injuries despite employing only around 7% of the workforce. The root causes are well documented:

- Inconsistent PPE compliance - workers frequently remove helmets, high-visibility vests, and safety harnesses when unsupervised.
- Inadequate hazard zone enforcement - restricted and danger zones are poorly enforced, especially on large, complex sites.
- Reactive safety management - incidents are addressed after the fact, rather than prevented proactively.
- Manual supervisor oversight - human supervisors cannot monitor every zone simultaneously and suffer from fatigue and attention drift.
- Incomplete and retrospective reporting - safety reports are compiled manually and lack granularity or real-time visibility.

## **1.2 Solution Overview**

SafeSight AI addresses these challenges through a three-layer architecture: an edge AI inference layer (running on-site), a cloud processing and storage layer, and a client application layer (web portal and mobile apps). The system operates 24/7, processes video at up to 30 frames per second per camera, and delivers detection-to-alert latency under 3 seconds.

# **2\. Project Scope & Objectives**

## **2.1 In Scope**

- Real-time PPE detection: hard hat, safety vest (high-visibility), safety harness, safety gloves, safety boots, protective eyewear, face masks/respirators
- Dangerous zone / geofencing violation detection and real-time alerts
- Unsafe behavior recognition: using mobile phone while operating machinery, running in restricted areas, unauthorized climbing, workers entering under suspended loads
- Crowd density monitoring and unsafe congregation detection
- Fall detection and near-miss event detection
- Fire and smoke detection (early warning)
- Multi-site management from a single centralized dashboard
- Role-based access control (RBAC): Super Admin, Site Manager, Safety Officer, Supervisor, Executive Viewer
- Real-time alert delivery: in-app push notification, SMS, email, PA system integration, on-site siren/light beacon trigger
- Comprehensive analytics: compliance trends, risk heatmaps, violation frequency, shift-level reporting
- Video evidence management: auto-clip extraction, secure cloud storage, tamper-evident metadata
- Regulatory compliance reporting: export to formats required by Vietnamese Ministry of Labour, OSHA, ISO 45001
- REST API and webhook infrastructure for third-party integration (ERP, BIM, HR systems)
- Mobile applications: iOS and Android (React Native) for supervisors and safety officers
- Edge computing support: NVIDIA Jetson Orin, Intel NUC, standard x86 servers with GPU
- On-premise deployment option for clients with data sovereignty requirements

## **2.2 Out of Scope (Phase 1)**

- Biometric identification of individual workers (facial recognition) - deferred to Phase 2 due to privacy regulation considerations
- Autonomous drone surveillance integration - Phase 3 roadmap
- AR/VR safety training modules - separate product line
- Predictive accident modeling using historical big data - Phase 2 roadmap

## **2.3 Strategic Objectives**

| **#** | **Objective**               | **Success Metric**                  | **Target**   |
| ----- | --------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | ------------ |
| O-01  | Maximize detection accuracy | PPE detection mAP@0.5               | \>= 92%      |
| O-02  | Minimize alert latency      | Detection-to-notification time      | < 3 seconds  |
| O-03  | Ensure system reliability   | Platform uptime (annual)            | \>= 99.5%    |
| O-04  | Drive adoption              | Active sites within 12 months       | \>= 50 sites |
| O-05  | Demonstrate safety ROI      | Avg. client PPE violation reduction | \>= 70%      |
| O-06  | Support scale               | Cameras per deployment (max)        | \>= 500      |
| O-07  | Maintain compliance         | Reports passing regulatory audit    | 100%         |

# **3\. Stakeholders & User Personas**

## **3.1 Stakeholder Map**

| **Stakeholder**                  | **Role**               | **Primary Interest**                                     | **Engagement Level** |
| -------------------------------- | ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
| Construction Company Executives  | Budget decision-makers | ROI, liability reduction, brand reputation               | High - sponsor       |
| Site Safety Officers (HSE)       | Primary end users      | Real-time alerts, compliance reports, violation evidence | High - daily use     |
| Site Supervisors / Foremen       | Operational end users  | Zone monitoring, team compliance, shift summaries        | High - daily use     |
| IT/Infrastructure Teams          | Technical integrators  | API reliability, security, on-premise deployment         | Medium - integration |
| Workers / Labour Force           | Subjects of monitoring | Fairness, privacy, non-punitive use                      | Low - indirect       |
| Regulatory Bodies (MoLISA, OSHA) | Compliance authorities | Accurate audit trails, standardised reports              | Medium - compliance  |
| Insurance Providers              | Risk assessors         | Incident data, compliance history                        | Low - reporting      |
| SafeSight AI Ops Team            | Platform operators     | System health, AI model quality, support SLAs            | High - internal      |

## **3.2 User Personas**

### **Persona 1 - Nguyen Van An, Site Safety Officer (HSE Manager)**

| **HSE Manager Profile**                                                                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Age: 38 \| Experience: 12 years in construction safety \| Tech proficiency: Medium                                                               |
| Primary device: Desktop in site office + Android smartphone on-site                                                                              |
| Goals: Achieve zero lost-time injuries \| Pass MoLISA quarterly inspections \| Maintain documented evidence of all violations                    |
| Pain points: Cannot watch all 40+ cameras simultaneously \| Violation reports take 4 hours to compile manually \| Workers ignore verbal warnings |
| Needs from SafeSight AI: Instant mobile alerts when violations occur \| One-click evidence download \| Automated weekly compliance report        |

### **Persona 2 - Tran Thi Bich, Executive Director (Construction Firm)**

| **Executive Profile**                                                                                                                                |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Age: 52 \| Experience: 20+ years in construction management \| Tech proficiency: Low-Medium                                                          |
| Primary device: iPad, minimal time on platform                                                                                                       |
| Goals: Protect company from legal liability \| Reduce accident-related insurance premiums \| Win safety-certified project bids                       |
| Pain points: Safety data is siloed and hard to interpret \| No visibility into cross-site trends \| Reactive - learns about incidents after the fact |
| Needs from SafeSight AI: High-level executive dashboard \| Cross-site KPI summaries \| Monthly board-ready safety reports                            |

### **Persona 3 - Pham Minh Duc, Site Foreman**

| **Foreman Profile**                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Age: 29 \| Experience: 5 years as foreman \| Tech proficiency: Medium                                                                            |
| Primary device: iPhone on-site                                                                                                                   |
| Goals: Keep crew safe \| Meet daily work targets without safety stoppages \| Handle violations quickly without escalation                        |
| Pain points: Workers remove PPE when hot/uncomfortable \| Hard to enforce rules in remote corners of large sites \| Paperwork is time-consuming  |
| Needs from SafeSight AI: Instant push notifications with camera snapshot \| Quick acknowledgement workflow \| Worker violation history per shift |

# **4\. Functional Requirements**

## **4.1 AI Detection Engine**

### **4.1.1 PPE Detection Module**

The core detection pipeline uses a YOLOv8/v9 object detection backbone fine-tuned on a proprietary dataset of construction site imagery (minimum 150,000 annotated images across diverse lighting, weather, and PPE type conditions).

| **Feature ID** | **Feature**              | **Description**                                                                                                                                           | **Priority**  |
| -------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| DET-001        | Hard Hat Detection       | Detect presence/absence of safety helmet per person bounding box. Distinguish between correct wearing and improper wearing (tilted, backward, on ground). | P0 - Critical |
| DET-002        | Safety Vest Detection    | Detect high-visibility vest, reflective jacket. Flag missing or partially worn vest.                                                                      | P0 - Critical |
| DET-003        | Safety Harness Detection | Detect full-body harness for workers at height (>2m). Detect harness-not-clipped events.                                                                  | P0 - Critical |
| DET-004        | Protective Eyewear       | Detect safety glasses or goggles in relevant work zones (welding, grinding, chemical).                                                                    | P1 - High     |
| DET-005        | Safety Gloves            | Detect gloves in high-risk manual tasks zone.                                                                                                             | P1 - High     |
| DET-006        | Safety Footwear          | Detect safety boots vs. non-compliant footwear (sandals, trainers).                                                                                       | P1 - High     |
| DET-007        | Respiratory Protection   | Detect dust masks, respirators in designated zones (demolition, concrete cutting).                                                                        | P1 - High     |
| DET-008        | Multi-PPE Simultaneous   | Simultaneously detect all PPE items per worker in a single inference pass.                                                                                | P0 - Critical |
| DET-009        | Compliance Score         | Calculate per-worker and per-zone PPE compliance score as a weighted percentage.                                                                          | P1 - High     |

### **4.1.2 Hazard Zone & Geofencing Module**

| **Feature ID** | **Feature**              | **Description**                                                                                                              | **Priority**  |
| -------------- | ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| ZONE-001       | Virtual Geofence Drawing | Admin can draw polygon or rectangular restricted zones directly on the camera feed or floor plan. Up to 50 zones per camera. | P0 - Critical |
| ZONE-002       | Real-Time Zone Violation | Detect when a person (or vehicle) enters a restricted zone. Trigger alert within 3 seconds.                                  | P0 - Critical |
| ZONE-003       | Zone Scheduling          | Time-based zone activation (e.g., Zone A is restricted only during night shift). Full calendar scheduling.                   | P1 - High     |
| ZONE-004       | Vehicle Proximity Alert  | Detect workers within a configurable radius of heavy machinery in motion.                                                    | P0 - Critical |
| ZONE-005       | Suspended Load Zone      | Auto-define exclusion zone beneath crane lift operations. Trigger alert for any personnel entry.                             | P0 - Critical |
| ZONE-006       | Zone Heatmap             | Generate time-lapse heatmap of zone violation frequency for weekly review.                                                   | P2 - Medium   |

### **4.1.3 Behavior Recognition Module**

| **Feature ID** | **Feature**           | **Description**                                                                                            | **Priority**  |
| -------------- | --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| BEH-001        | Phone Use Detection   | Detect mobile phone usage by operator/worker in restricted-use zones.                                      | P1 - High     |
| BEH-002        | Running Detection     | Detect running gait in restricted-speed zones.                                                             | P1 - High     |
| BEH-003        | Fall Detection        | Detect sudden posture change indicative of a fall event. Trigger emergency alert.                          | P0 - Critical |
| BEH-004        | Unauthorized Climbing | Detect climbing on scaffolding, structures, or equipment in non-designated areas.                          | P1 - High     |
| BEH-005        | Crowd Density Alert   | Alert when the number of people in a zone exceeds a configured threshold.                                  | P1 - High     |
| BEH-006        | Lone Worker Alert     | Alert when a single worker is detected alone in a high-risk zone for more than a configurable time period. | P1 - High     |
| BEH-007        | Near-Miss Detection   | Detect close-call events (e.g., object narrowly missed, worker stumbled) for retrospective analysis.       | P2 - Medium   |

### **4.1.4 Environmental Hazard Detection**

| **Feature ID** | **Feature**                    | **Description**                                                                     | **Priority**  |
| -------------- | ------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| ENV-001        | Fire & Smoke Detection         | Computer vision smoke/flame detection as early-warning layer alongside IoT sensors. | P0 - Critical |
| ENV-002        | Spill / Puddle Detection       | Detect liquid spills or standing water in walkway zones (slip hazard).              | P2 - Medium   |
| ENV-003        | Debris / Obstruction Detection | Detect unsecured debris or materials in evacuation routes.                          | P2 - Medium   |

## **4.2 Camera & Video Management**

| **Feature ID** | **Feature**                  | **Description**                                                                                                     | **Priority**  |
| -------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |
| CAM-001        | ONVIF Protocol Support       | Support all ONVIF-compliant IP cameras. Also support RTSP streams.                                                  | P0 - Critical |

## **4.6 Integration & API Layer**

| **Feature ID** | **Feature**               | **Description**                                                                                                                       | **Priority**  |
| -------------- | ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------- |

| API-002        | Webhook Subscriptions     | Subscribe to event types (violation_detected, alert_escalated, camera_offline). Retry logic with exponential backoff.                 | P0 - Critical |
| API-003        | ERP Integration Connector | Pre-built connectors for SAP, Oracle, and Microsoft Dynamics for safety incident ingestion.                                           | P2 - Medium   |

| API-006        | IoT Sensor Integration    | Ingest data from gas sensors, temperature sensors, and wearable IoT devices for correlated alerting.                                  | P2 - Medium   |

# **5\. Non-Functional Requirements**

## **5.1 Performance Requirements**

| **ID**   | **Requirement**             | **Target**                                                              |
| -------- | --------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| PERF-001 | AI Inference Latency (edge) | < 100ms per frame per camera stream at 1080p                            |
| PERF-002 | Detection-to-Alert Delivery | < 3 seconds end-to-end from violation event to notification receipt     |
| PERF-003 | Concurrent Camera Streams   | Single edge node processes >= 16 x 1080p streams at 15 FPS              |
| PERF-004 | Dashboard Load Time         | < 2 seconds initial load; < 500ms subsequent page transitions           |
| PERF-005 | API Response Time           | P95 response time < 200ms for all read endpoints under normal load      |
| PERF-006 | Report Generation           | < 30 seconds for reports covering up to 90-day range and 100 cameras    |
| PERF-007 | Video Clip Retrieval        | Signed URL available for clip download < 5 seconds after request        |
| PERF-008 | Maximum Cameras / Platform  | \>= 5,000 cameras across all deployments on shared cloud infrastructure |

## **5.2 Reliability & Availability**

| **ID**  | **Requirement**          | **Target**                                                                                      |
| ------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| REL-001 | Platform Uptime SLA      | \>= 99.5% measured monthly (< 3.6 hours downtime/month)                                         |
| REL-002 | Edge Node Resilience     | Edge node continues local detection and buffering for >= 72 hours if cloud connectivity is lost |
| REL-003 | Alert Delivery Guarantee | Alert delivery success rate >= 99.9% (retry logic + fallback channels)                          |
| REL-004 | Data Durability          | Video evidence and violation records stored with >= 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability         |
| REL-005 | Graceful Degradation     | If AI inference is overloaded, system queues frames and alerts operator - never silently drops  |
| REL-006 | RTO                      | Recovery Time Objective: < 1 hour for full platform restoration after major incident            |
| REL-007 | RPO                      | Recovery Point Objective: < 5 minutes data loss in disaster recovery scenario                   |

## **5.3 Security Requirements**

| **ID**  | **Requirement**          | **Detail**                                                                                                        |
| ------- | ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| SEC-001 | Encryption in Transit    | TLS 1.3 for all API, WebSocket, and dashboard communications. SRTP for RTSP streams.                              |
| SEC-002 | Encryption at Rest       | AES-256 encryption for all stored video, violation records, and configuration data.                               |
| SEC-003 | Penetration Testing      | Annual third-party penetration test. Critical findings remediated within 14 days.                                 |
| SEC-004 | Vulnerability Management | CVE scanning on all container images in CI/CD pipeline. No Critical/High CVEs in production.                      |
| SEC-005 | API Authentication       | OAuth 2.0 Bearer tokens with short expiry (15 min access token, 7-day refresh token).                             |
| SEC-006 | Data Privacy             | Compliance with Vietnamese Decree 13/2023/ND-CP on personal data protection. No biometric data stored in Phase 1. |
| SEC-007 | Network Isolation        | Edge node communicates with cloud via VPN tunnel. Camera VLAN isolated from corporate network.                    |
| SEC-008 | Audit Logging            | All administrative actions and data access logged to immutable append-only store (WORM).                          |

## **5.4 Scalability Requirements**

- Horizontal auto-scaling of inference workers, API services, and stream processors via Kubernetes (K8s) HPA.
- AI model serving layer supports GPU-accelerated nodes (NVIDIA A10/A100) for cloud inference.
- Message queue architecture (Apache Kafka) for decoupled, high-throughput event processing.
- Database: PostgreSQL with read replicas; TimescaleDB for time-series violation metrics; Redis for session and cache.
- Object storage: S3-compatible (AWS S3 or MinIO for on-premise) with automated tiered lifecycle policies.
- Multi-region support: Primary region Vietnam (AWS ap-southeast-1); DR region Singapore.

# **6\. System Architecture**

## **6.1 Architecture Overview**

SafeSight AI is built on a three-tier architecture comprising the Edge Layer, Cloud Backend Layer, and Client Application Layer. All tiers communicate over encrypted channels and are designed for independent horizontal scaling.

## **6.2 Edge Layer (On-Site Hardware)**

### **6.2.1 Edge Node Specifications**

| **Component**     | **Specification**                                                             | **Purpose**                     |
| ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------- |
| Primary Hardware  | NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin 64GB or equivalent x86 server with NVIDIA RTX 4000 GPU | AI inference, stream processing |
| OS                | Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (headless)                                                   | Edge OS base                    |
| Inference Runtime | TensorRT 8.x, ONNX Runtime with CUDA                                          | Optimized model execution       |
| Stream Ingestor   | GStreamer pipeline + custom Python orchestrator                               | RTSP stream handling            |
| Local Storage     | 2TB NVMe SSD (RAID-1)                                                         | 30-day local video buffer       |
| Connectivity      | 1 Gbps LAN + 4G LTE failover                                                  | Cloud sync and alert delivery   |
| Watchdog Service  | Systemd + custom health-check daemon                                          | Auto-restart on failure         |

### **6.2.2 Edge Processing Pipeline**

- RTSP streams ingested per camera via GStreamer.
- Frames decoded and batched at configurable FPS (default: 10 FPS, up to 30 FPS).
- Batched frames passed to TensorRT-optimized YOLOv8 inference engine.
- Detection bounding boxes and class labels output per frame.
- Post-processing module applies NMS, confidence thresholding, and tracking (ByteTrack algorithm).
- Rule engine evaluates detections against configured zone rules and PPE requirements.
- Violation events published to local Kafka broker for guaranteed delivery.
- Events forwarded to cloud backend via gRPC over VPN. Buffered locally on connectivity loss.
- Violation video clip extracted and uploaded to cloud object storage asynchronously.

## **6.3 Cloud Backend Layer**

| **Service**              | **Technology**                    | **Responsibility**                                |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| API Gateway              | AWS API Gateway + Kong            | Rate limiting, auth, routing, load balancing      |
| Core API Service         | Python FastAPI (async)            | Business logic, CRUD, report generation           |
| Event Processing Service | Apache Kafka + Kafka Streams      | Real-time violation event ingestion and fanout    |
| Alert Dispatch Service   | Celery + Redis (async workers)    | Multi-channel alert delivery with retry logic     |
| AI Training Pipeline     | MLflow + PyTorch + AWS SageMaker  | Model retraining, versioning, A/B evaluation      |
| Video Storage            | AWS S3 + CloudFront CDN           | Secure violation clip storage and delivery        |
| Primary Database         | PostgreSQL 15 (RDS) + TimescaleDB | Relational data, time-series metrics              |
| Cache Layer              | Redis 7 Cluster                   | Session, frequently-accessed configs, rate limits |
| Search / Audit Log       | OpenSearch (AWS)                  | Full-text search on logs; WORM audit trail        |
| Container Orchestration  | Kubernetes (EKS) + Helm           | Service deployment, scaling, health management    |
| Observability            | Prometheus + Grafana + PagerDuty  | Metrics, dashboards, on-call alerting             |

## **6.4 Client Application Layer**

| **Application**      | **Technology**                       | **Key Capabilities**                                                            |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Web Portal (Primary) | React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS | Full platform management, dashboards, analytics, configuration, video review    |
| iOS App              | React Native (Expo) + Swift modules  | Real-time alerts, mobile camera view, incident acknowledgement, field reporting |
| Android App          | React Native (Expo) + Kotlin modules | Same as iOS; FCM push notifications; offline capability for alert history       |
| Edge Admin Console   | Vue 3 (local, no internet required)  | On-site edge node configuration, camera management, local health monitoring     |

# **7\. AI Model Specifications**

## **7.1 Detection Models**

| **Model**           | **Architecture**              | **Dataset**                                    | **mAP@0.5 Target**   | **Inference (ms)** |
| ------------------- | ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------ |

## **7.2 Model Training & Continuous Improvement**

- Training Infrastructure: AWS SageMaker with NVIDIA A100 GPU instances.
- Data Annotation Pipeline: LabelStudio annotation platform with QA review workflow. All annotations validated by senior annotators.
- Dataset Versioning: DVC (Data Version Control) for full dataset reproducibility.
- Model Registry: MLflow model registry with staging / production promotion workflow.
- Active Learning Loop: Low-confidence detections from production are automatically queued for human review and re-annotation to continuously improve model quality.
- Retraining Frequency: Scheduled monthly retraining + ad-hoc retraining triggered by mAP degradation alerts.
- A/B Evaluation: New model versions are shadow-deployed against production traffic before full promotion. Promotion requires demonstrating >= 1% mAP improvement on holdout set.
- Bias Monitoring: Model performance is evaluated across sub-groups (time of day, weather condition, PPE colour variations) to detect and mitigate performance disparities.

## **7.3 Model Explainability**

- Grad-CAM heatmap visualizations available for any detection event in the web portal, allowing safety officers to understand what the AI analyzed.
- Confidence scores displayed alongside all detections and violation events.
- Per-class precision/recall metrics published on a public model card updated with each release.

# SafeSight AI вҖ” Edge Layer Technical Specification

**Document Version:** 1.0
**Status:** Approved for Implementation
**Date:** April 2026
**Classification:** Confidential
**Owner:** Edge AI Team вҖ” SafeSight AI

---

## 1. Overview & Scope

This document defines the complete technical specification for the SafeSight AI Edge Layer вҖ” the on-site compute and software subsystem that performs AI inference, event detection, local buffering, and cloud synchronization for the SafeSight AI construction safety platform.

**Design philosophy:** Edge-first. The edge node must operate fully autonomously during cloud outages, buffer all violation events, and synchronize transparently on reconnect. No violation is silently dropped.

**Key targets (from PRD В§5.1, В§6.2):**

| Requirement | Target |
|---|---|
| AI inference latency | < 100ms per frame per stream at 1080p |
| Concurrent streams | вүҘ 16 Г— 1080p at 15 FPS per edge node |
| End-to-end detection-to-alert | < 3 seconds |
| Offline buffer duration | вүҘ 72 hours |
| Auto-clip on event | 30s clip (15s pre + 15s post) |
| Bandwidth fallover | 4G LTE with adaptive quality |

---

## 2. Hardware Specifications

### 2.1 Primary Platform: NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin 64GB

| Power | 60W barrel jack or PoE++ via carrier board | Industrial PSU with UPS backup |
| OS | Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (headless) | JetPack 6.0 / L4T 36.x |

### 2.3 Carrier Board & Enclosure

## 4. GStreamer RTSP Pipeline Architecture

### 4.1 Pipeline Design

Each camera stream runs as an independent GStreamer pipeline. Pipelines are managed by a Python orchestrator (`pipeline_manager.py`) using the Gst-python bindings.

**Canonical pipeline (RTSP вҶ’ decode вҶ’ batch вҶ’ output queue):**

```
rtspsrc location=rtsp://<user>:<pass>@<ip>:<port>/<path>
    name=src_<cam_id>
    latency=200
    drop-on-latency=true
  ! rtph264depay
  ! avdec_h264
    name=dec_<cam_id>
    error-diffusion=true
  ! video/x-raw,format=I420
  ! videoscale
    method=nearest-neighbor
    add-borders=false
  ! video/x-raw,format=I420,width=1920,height=1080,pixel-aspect-ratio=1/1,framerate=15/1
  ! videorate
    drop-only=true
  ! queue
    max-size-buffers=5
    max-size-bytes=0
    max-size-time=0
  ! appsink
    name=sink_<cam_id>
    emit-signals=true
    max-buffers=1
    drop=true
    sync=false
```

**Configuration parameters per camera:**

| Parameter | Default | Range | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `fps` | 15 | 5вҖ“30 | Configurable per camera |
| `resolution` | 1080p | 720pвҖ“4K | Downscale to 1080p if source is higher |
| `latency` | 200ms | 50вҖ“500ms | Buffer to handle network jitter |
| `decode-threads` | 4 | 1вҖ“8 | HW decode preferred |
| `drop-on-latency` | true | bool | Prevent unbounded buffering |

### 4.2 Pipeline Manager

`pipeline_manager.py` manages pipeline lifecycle:

```python
class PipelineManager:
    def __init__(self, max_concurrent: int = 16):
        self.max_concurrent = max_concurrent
        self.pipelines: dict[str, Gst.Pipeline] = {}
        self.cam_configs: dict[str, CameraConfig] = {}

    def add_camera(self, cam_id: str, rtsp_url: str, config: CameraConfig) -> None
    def remove_camera(self, cam_id: str) -> None
    def restart_pipeline(self, cam_id: str) -> None  # on stream error
    def get_frame_rate(self, cam_id: str) -> float    # measured, not configured
    def health_check(self) -> dict[str, PipelineHealth]
```

### 4.3 Stream Health Monitoring

Each pipeline emits `fps-measured` and `drop-rate` signals. The manager exposes Prometheus metrics:

```
# HELP safesight_rtsp_fps Camera stream FPS
# TYPE safesight_rtsp_fps gauge
safesight_rtsp_fps{camera_id="CAM-001"} 14.8

# HELP safesight_rtsp_drop_rate Dropped frames per second
# TYPE safesight_rtsp_drop_rate gauge
safesight_rtsp_drop_rate{camera_id="CAM-001"} 0.2

# HELP safesight_rtsp_latency_ms Estimated end-to-end latency
# TYPE safesight_rtsp_latency_ms gauge
safesight_rtsp_latency_ms{camera_id="CAM-001"} 185
```

## 5. TensorRT Inference Engine

### 5.1 Model Artifacts

| Model | Architecture | Input Size | INT8 Calib. | Target ms (Orin) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| PPE Detector | YOLOv8-L | 640Г—640 | Yes | < 40ms |
| Behavior Recognizer | YOLOv8-Pose + LSTM | 640Г—640 | Yes | < 60ms |
| Zone Monitor | YOLOv8-M | 640Г—640 | Yes | < 30ms |
| Fire/Smoke | EfficientDet-D2 | 512Г—512 | Yes | < 50ms |

Model artifacts are stored at: `/opt/safesight/models/`

```
models/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ppe_yolov8l_fp16.engine     # compiled TensorRT engine
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ppe_yolov8l_int8.calib     # INT8 calibration cache
в”ңв”Җв”Җ ppe_classes.txt             # class ID вҶ’ label mapping
в”ңв”Җв”Җ behavior_yolov8pose_fp16.engine
в”ңв”Җв”Җ zone_yolov8m_fp16.engine
в”ңв”Җв”Җ firesmoke_effdetd2_fp16.engine
в””в”Җв”Җ models.manifest.json        # version, checksum, metadata
```

### 5.2 TensorRT Engine Setup (`tensorrt_engine.py`)

```python
class TensorRTEngine:
    def __init__(
        self,
        engine_path: str,
        conf_thresh: float = 0.35,
        iou_thresh: float = 0.5,
        max_det: int = 100,
    ):
        self.conf_thresh = conf_thresh
        self.iou_thresh = iou_thresh
        self.engine = self._load_engine(engine_path)
        self.context = self.engine.create_execution_context()
        self.bindings = self._allocate_buffers()
        self.stream = cuda.Stream()

    def infer(self, blob: np.ndarray) -> list[Detection]:
        """Run inference on a single frame blob (CHW, BGR, 640x640)."""
        ...

    def infer_batch(self, blobs: list[np.ndarray]) -> list[list[Detection]]:
        """Batch inference for throughput optimization."""
        ...

    def warmup(self, iterations: int = 50) -> None:
        """Run dummy inference to initialize CUDA context and engine."""
        ...

    def get_input_spec(self) -> tuple[int, int, int]:  # C, H, W
        return (3, 640, 640)
```

### 5.3 INT8 Quantization

- Calibration dataset: 5,000 representative frames from operational cameras
- Use NVIDIA's `polygraphy` tool for calibration and validation
- FP16 is the default precision; INT8 only deployed after accuracy validation (mAP drop < 1%)
- Engine file includes embedded calibration cache вҖ” no runtime calibration needed

### 5.4 Inference Scheduling

- **Batch mode**: Accumulate frames from up to 4 cameras into a single batch (up to 4Г—640Г—640)
- **Max batch size**: 8 (Orin AGX), dynamically sized by available GPU memory
- **CUDA streams**: One stream per camera for concurrent processing
- **Dynamic batch sizing**: Adjust batch size based on GPU utilization (target 70вҖ“85%)

---

## 6. YOLOv8/v9 Model Integration

### 6.1 Model Training & Export Pipeline

Models are trained in cloud (AWS SageMaker, PyTorch), then exported to ONNX and compiled to TensorRT.

```bash
# Export ONNX from PyTorch (YOLOv8)
yolo export model=yolov8l.pt format=onnx opset=12 imgsz=640 batch=8

# Validate ONNX accuracy before TRT compilation
polygraphy run yolov8l.onnx --onnxrt --val-yolov8-pipe

# Compile to TensorRT FP16
trtexec \
  --onnx=yolov8l.onnx \
  --engine=yolov8l_fp16.engine \
  --fp16 \
  --workspace=8192 \
  --timing-cache=yolov8l_cache.cache \
  --shapes=images:0:8x3x640x640

# INT8 calibration
trtexec \
  --onnx=yolov8l.onnx \
  --engine=yolov8l_int8.engine \
  --int8 \
  --calib=yolov8l_calib_input/ \
  --workspace=8192 \
  --shapes=images:0:8x3x640x640
```

### 6.2 Post-Processing (NMS + BBox Decode)

Custom CUDA kernel for NMS (non-maximum suppression) on GPU:

```python
def postprocess(
    output: np.ndarray,   # (batch, num_dets, 85) вҖ” YOLOv8 format
    conf_thresh: float = 0.35,
    iou_thresh: float = 0.5,
    max_det: int = 100,
) -> list[Detection]:
    """
    1. Filter by confidence
    2. Decode xywh вҶ’ xyxy bboxes
    3. CUDA NMS
    4. Return top-max_det detections per image
    """
```

### 6.3 Class Definitions


## 7. ByteTrack Multi-Object Tracking

### 7.1 Architecture

ByteTrack is used as the MOT (Multi-Object Tracking) backend. Each detection is classified as HIGH or LOW confidence and associated to existing tracklets. Low-confidence detections are kept to recover occluded tracks.

```
Detection tracks (high conf) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                                  в”ңв”Җв”Җв–¶ Data Association (Hungarian / ByteTrack)
Detection tracks (low conf) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                                  в”Ӯ
                                  в–ј
                          Tracklet Manager
                          (ID lifecycle:
                           new вҶ’ tracked вҶ’ lost вҶ’ removed)
```

### 7.2 Configuration

```python
class ByteTrackConfig:
    track_thresh: float = 0.5        # detection threshold for new tracks
    track_buffer: int = 30            # frames to keep lost tracks
    match_thresh: float = 0.8         # IoU threshold for matching
    second_match_thresh: float = 0.5  # match low-conf detections
    min_box_area: float = 100         # filter tiny boxes
    max_time_lost: int = 30           # frames before track considered lost
    frame_rate: float = 15.0          # used for time-to-lost calculation
```

### 7.3 Tracklet Data

```python
@dataclass
class Tracklet:
    track_id: int
    detections: list[Detection]       # last N smoothed detections
    centroid_history: list[tuple[int, int]]
    class_counts: dict[int, int]      # class_id вҶ’ count (for PPE rule)
    first_seen: float                  # timestamp
    last_seen: float                   # timestamp
    state: Literal["new", "tracked", "lost", "removed"]
    current_ppe_compliance: PPEComplianceScore
```

### 7.4 PPE Compliance Score Per Tracklet

Tracks accumulate PPE detections over a sliding window (last 5 frames). The compliance score is computed per-class:

```
compliance[class_id] = max(1, detections_of_class) / window_size * 100
```

Violation is triggered when:
- `compliance[person] == 1` (person detected) AND
- `compliance[hard_hat] < threshold` (configurable, default 0.6) AND
- violation_confirmed_for >= 2 consecutive seconds

```python
            if matched:

                if not self._is_suppressed(event):
                    events.append(event)
                    self._record_violation(event)
```

## 10. Video Clip Extraction (30s: 15s Pre + 15s Post)

### 10.1 Architecture

Clip extraction is event-triggered, decoupled from the inference pipeline, and runs as an independent service (`clip_extractor.py`) consuming from `safesight.events.clip` Kafka topic.

**Pre-event buffer:** A 30-second ring buffer of decoded H.264 frames is maintained per camera in a circular memory-mapped file (MMF) on NVMe.

### 10.2 Ring Buffer Design

```python
class CircularFrameBuffer:
    """
    Mmap-backed ring buffer: 30 seconds Г— 15 FPS = 450 frames per camera.
    Each frame: ~1920Г—1080 JPEG @ Q80 вүҲ 80 KB вҶ’ ~36 MB per camera buffer.
    For 16 cameras: ~576 MB total.
    """
    def __init__(self, camera_id: str, capacity_sec: int = 30, fps: float = 15.0):
        self.capacity = int(capacity_sec * fps)
        self.frame_size = 1920 * 1080 * 3  # approximate
        self.mmap = mmap.mmap(-1, self.capacity * self.frame_size,
                               mmap_name=f"safesight_buffer_{camera_id}")
        self.meta = RingMeta(camera_id, self.capacity)  # separate shm

    def push(self, frame: np.ndarray, timestamp: float) -> None:
        idx = self.meta.write_idx % self.capacity
        self._write_frame(idx, frame)
        self.meta.timestamps[idx] = timestamp
        self.meta.write_idx += 1

    def get_clip(self, event_timestamp: float, pre_sec: float = 15.0, post_sec: float = 15.0
    ) -> tuple[list[np.ndarray], list[float]]:
        """Return frames and timestamps for clip window."""
        ...
```

## 12. Cloud Sync & Reconnection Logic

### 12.1 Sync Protocol

Events are synchronized to the cloud backend via gRPC over a TLS VPN tunnel. Kafka messages from the `safesight.events.violation` topic are forwarded to the cloud Kafka cluster.

```
Edge Kafka в”Җв”Җв–¶ Kafka Bridge Service в”Җв”Җв–¶ VPN Tunnel в”Җв”Җв–¶ Cloud Kafka в”Җв”Җв–¶ Cloud API
                              в”Ӯ                        в”Ӯ
                              в–ј                        в–ј
                        Local retry queue         Cloud acknowledgement
```

### 12.2 Reconnection Logic (`cloud_sync.py`)

```python
class CloudSyncManager:
    def __init__(self, config: CloudConfig):
        self.config = config
        self.state = SyncState.DISCONNECTED
        self.pending_buffer: deque[ViolationEvent] = deque(maxlen=100_000)
        self.reconnect_strategy = ExponentialBackoff(
            base_delay=5, max_delay=300, jitter=0.1
        )
        self.current_delay = self.reconnect_strategy.base_delay

    async def connect(self) -> None:
        try:
            await self.grpc_channel.connect()
            await self._handshake()
            self.state = SyncState.CONNECTED
            self.current_delay = self.reconnect_strategy.base_delay
            await self._flush_pending()
        except Exception as e:
            await self._handle_disconnect(e)

    async def _handle_disconnect(self, error: Exception) -> None:
        self.state = SyncState.RECONNECTING
        logger.warning(f"Cloud sync disconnected: {error}")
        await self._buffer_pending()
        await asyncio.sleep(self.current_delay)
        self.current_delay = min(
            self.current_delay * 2,
            self.reconnect_strategy.max_delay
        )
        await self.connect()

    async def _buffer_pending(self) -> None:
        # All in-flight events already in local Kafka; no additional buffering needed
        pass

    async def _flush_pending(self) -> None:
        # Replay events from local Kafka topic starting from last confirmed offset
        last_offset = await self._get_last_confirmed_offset()
        async for event in self.kafka_consumer.seek_to(last_offset):
            await self.send_event(event)
```

### 12.3 Bandwidth Management

| Scenario | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Primary (1 Gbps LAN) | Full throughput, all event metadata, clip uploads at full quality |
| LTE failover | Reduce to critical alerts only; clips stored locally until LAN restored |
| LTE low-signal | Adaptive bitrate on clips; batch non-critical sync; sync during off-hours |
| Offline (>72h) | Continue local operation; alert via local siren/PA; flag data for mandatory sync on reconnect |

### 12.4 Clip Upload Strategy

- Clips are uploaded asynchronously via presigned S3 URLs (obtained from cloud API on-demand)
- Upload uses `rsync`-style delta: only chunks not already on cloud
- Upload retry: exponential backoff, max 5 retries
- Clips older than 24 hours not uploaded via LTE (defer to LAN restoration)

### 13.2 Watchdog Service (`watchdog.py`)

The watchdog is the master orchestrator. It runs as a systemd service and performs:

```python
class Watchdog:
    def __init__(self):
        self.services = [
            "safesight-pipeline",
            "safesight-inference",
            "safesight-kafka",
            "safesight-sync",
            "safesight-clip",
            "safesight-admin",
        ]
        self.health = HealthMonitor()
        self.prometheus = PrometheusMetrics()

    async def run(self) -> None:
        while True:
            status = await self._check_all_services()
            await self.health.publish(status)

            if status.critical:
                await self._handle_critical(status)
            elif status.degraded:
                await self._auto_heal(status)

            await asyncio.sleep(10)  # Health check every 10s

    async def _check_all_services(self) -> NodeHealth:
        ...
```

| Kafka broker | Port 9092 not listening; produce test message | Restart broker; buffer events in memory |
| Clip extractor | Queue depth > 500 for > 5 min | Increase worker count; alert |
| Cloud sync | gRPC connection lost for > 60s | Trigger reconnect; log pending count |

## 14. Bandwidth Optimization (4G LTE Scenarios)

### 14.1 Adaptive Stream Quality

| Connection | FPS | Resolution | Model Precision |
|---|---|---|---|
| LAN (вүҘ 100 Mbps) | 15 | 1080p | FP16 |
| LTE (вүҘ 10 Mbps) | 10 | 1080p | FP16 |
| LTE (вүҘ 5 Mbps) | 8 | 720p | INT8 |
| LTE (< 5 Mbps) | 5 | 720p | INT8 |
| Emergency (no network) | 15 | 1080p | FP16 (local only) |

Auto-negotiation based on measured throughput over a 60-second sliding window.

### 14.2 Intelligent Event Filtering

- **LAN**: All violations вҶ’ Kafka вҶ’ Cloud
- **LTE**: Only `severity=critical` violations вҶ’ direct gRPC (skip Kafka batching)
- **Emergency**: Zero outbound; local storage + siren/PA only

### 14.3 Clip Compression on LTE

| Target Bandwidth | Codec | Preset | CRF | Approx Bitrate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| вүҘ 2 Mbps sustained | H.264 | `veryfast` | 28 | ~1.5 Mbps |
| < 2 Mbps | H.265/HEVC | `veryfast` | 30 | ~0.8 Mbps |

### 14.4 Scheduled Bulk Sync

Non-critical events and pending clips are bulk-uploaded during configured low-traffic windows (e.g., 01:00вҖ“05:00 local time), using TCP BBR congestion control for efficient LTE utilization.

### 14.5 Data Budget Management

LTE data budget is configurable (default: 10 GB/month). When budget is:
- **> 50% remaining**: Normal operation
- **25вҖ“50%**: Disable clip auto-upload; sync only on Wi-Fi/LAN
- **< 25%**: Critical alerts only; log data consumption
- **0%**: LTE disabled; continue in local-only mode

### 15.2 Config Hot-Reload

All config files are watched via `inotify`. On change detection:
1. Validate new config (schema + business logic)
2. Apply to affected service without restart (where possible)
3. Log change with actor and timestamp
4. Push to cloud via `safesight.commands.config` Kafka topic

## 17. Performance Budget

| Operation | Budget | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| RTSP decode + queue | < 10ms | GStreamer hw decode |
| TensorRT inference (YOLOv8-L FP16) | < 40ms | Orin AGX @ 640Г—640 |
| Post-process NMS | < 5ms | CUDA kernel |
| ByteTrack association | < 3ms | Per camera, CPU |
| Rule evaluation | < 2ms | Per camera, in-memory |
| Kafka produce | < 5ms | Async, local broker |
| **Total per-frame latency** | **< 65ms** | Leaves 35ms headroom on 100ms target |
| 16-camera concurrent FPS | 15 FPS each | Total: 240 frames/sec |
| GPU utilization | 65вҖ“80% | Target range at max load |
| CPU utilization | < 60% | Inference offloaded to GPU |

---

## 18. Security

| Concern | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Camera credentials | Stored encrypted (AES-256-GCM) in `cameras.yaml.enc`, decrypted in memory only |
| Admin console auth | Bearer token, hashed in `secrets/admin_token` |
| Model integrity | SHA-256 checksum in `models.manifest.json`; verified on load |
| Kafka network | Listeners bound to `127.0.0.1` only; no external exposure |
| Clip metadata tampering | HMAC-SHA256 signature embedded in clip SEI NAL unit |
| Config tampering | Config signed with HMAC; watchdog validates signature on reload |
| VPN connectivity | TLS 1.3 to cloud; mutual TLS with client certificates |
